reviews.review_description: Robbed the game from the real developer and then ruined it ...now i see there's a remaster..

reviews.review_title: Guys, you should've done it  better.
reviews.review_description:
OK,folks.  Alterside failed and ruined everything. 
The game is totally unplayable, with no leaderboard and full waste of money.I am uninstalled from my vr.
It's a goddamn scam and thieft. Do not recommend it!

P.S.: alterside, u better stop sales,people won't buy it.And remember: u ain't scare us.

The game is broken, and it has no Leaderboards and no Custom songs. And it seems like a fraud to the people who get it because some players are competitive that they want custom songs and try to be the best in the world

I received this game 3 years ago and it no longer works correctly, the publisher, Alterside, apparently decided to ruin the game.

DO NOT PURCHASE THIS GAME!!

reviews.review_title: New devs stole and ruined this game
reviews.review_description: The new developer, Alterside, ruined the game with AI art. Won't be supporting unethical practices that tries to replace artists. Also, they stole it from the original dev. Very abhorrent.

Such a good game, highly recommend. The company who published the game, Alterside, has taken control of the game page and removed the real developers credit from the store page. Don't buy this game on the meta store!!!!!
Money hungry thieves stole someone else’s hard work, don’t buy this game
Great rhythm/workout game, unique esthetic, a lot of community levels
save your dollars

Of course it reminds me of Beat Saber, but in my opinion it’s better in some ways. I was surprised that for a game called BoomBox that the “drum beats” were little drums instead of little speakers and the “drum sticks” weren’t long notes. Looking like actual music 🎶 notes but stretched out in the form of a stick. Would have made a little more sense to me style wise. Maybe they will add a skin like that later. Overall I can’t wait until this gets popular enough to have even more music tracks. Maybe even some bigger artists. Like slushee, Tokyo machine, etc. I love boppy techno music. This game is worth it.

developer_response.body:
Hi DethBuny, thanks for your review, we are excited that you are enjoying our game.
We have taken note of your suggestions and passed them on to our creative team. Keep playing with us and maybe, in one of the next updates, you will find that we have followed up on your idea!

reviews.review_title: Superb workout!  And amazing price but..
reviews.review_description:
Could you fix the slight stuttering on the screen when you select your music? It happens when previewing a song? 

Do that and this will easily be a five star game. Its great! Oh and an option to remove the cat!! Love the workouts but not a cat person 🤣

Keep up the great work!!

developer_response.body:
Hey fonejackerjon,
Thank you for your feedback - we are very happy that you enjoy BoomBox!
We will take note of the buzzing issue, and will see how this can be improved (we have not noticed it ourselves before, so it is great that you pointed it out).
As for Pupa - you can dress her as a deer and we will be adding more customizations as soon as we are accepted by the official Quest store! There were some requests for dogs, fish and hamsters, which would be super fun :D

reviews.review_description:
I only spend money on VR games which get me moving intensively. This is a keeper!

Large selection of good songs, good mapping. Fitness value similar to Beat Saber or Audio Trip Cardio, but sufficiently different motion to warrant a place in my library.  

I am not particularly into cats, it would be nice to have a puppy option as well :)

developer_response.body:
Hi vgergo,

thank you for your feedback! We are so super happy that you like BoomBox - please, feel free to join our Discord in case you'd like to stay in touch with us and the community more closely.  
Some of our players mentioned a doggy as well - we totally understand various preferences. Once we are accepted to the official Quest store, we will definitely add more pets ^^

reviews.review_description:
only played this game for around half an hour so far, but it looks beautiful, appears to be a good workout and the cat is the cutest ( i dont even like cats!) 

The only thing i would change is the feedback when you hit a drum, not sure if i need  to change something in settings, but i would like more vibration feedback to make it more satisying when hitting the drums 

still giving this 5 stars though as its just beautiful

developer_response.body:
Hi GoreJuice, thank you for your review and feedback :3
You can change the feedback, color, and texture of the drums (and much more) in the menu (the cog settings icon is in the left top corner, next to the skins cat icon). We started working on the v2 of UI and hopefully, can deliver easier navigation by spring.

reviews.review_title: One of the best rhythm games out there!
reviews.review_description:
BoomBox is not "reinventing the wheel" in terms of rhythm games, but it definitely is making the wheel all shiny and new again, and it's an welcome addition to the Oculus Lab, that's for sure. 
You play drums to the beat using drumsticks instead of slashing through targets, and there are plenty of customizations to take advantage of, and achievements to earn, which makes the game more interesting. 

The best part has got to be the devs and the community.
I noticed a bug, reported it, and it was fixed within two days, after only mentioning it once. That's almost unheard of (at least in my experience).
There are a lot of community mappers uploading new maps regularly, and they have their own editor you can download to make maps yourself as well! 
Can't wait to see this game skyrocket in popularity, as it rightfully deserves 🚀📻🥁

developer_response.body: Hi EtherealDave, thanks for your review, we are excited that you are enjoying our game!

reviews.review_description:
First, thank you developers for this fun game, I enjoyed playing this every time.  
Want more if possible, more new environment map , more colour,  please.  An under water map if good idea.  Please continue to add more gameplay more colourful environment into the game if possible, please.  Thank you

developer_response.body: Hi bryanlo, thanks for your review, we are excited that you are enjoying our game!

reviews.review_description: It’s A Solid Game, It’s Fun, It Has Great Song, And It Is A Great Way To Exercise...

reviews.review_title: I did say I'd give ya 5 stars 😁
reviews.review_description: Just played this excellent drumming game. Good fun so far. It's got multiplayer, maps, and songs from the community, and it burns a few cals 😁. Downside I had to clap a cat 😂kidding

Yes, you did ;) It was great to meet you yesterday and we are super happy that you enjoy our game :3
Do not clap a cat haha

reviews.review_description:
update

I find myself not playing this game as much recently after the initial play test of a few days. The main issue is the satisfaction of hitting the drums, the sounds don't match up very well to the hits. The second issue is the scoring is not explained, there needs to be a indication of some kind to tell you the perfect time to hit the drum, right now it's a guess which seems to be close to the bottom of the stick instead of the tip of it. Some small changes would make this much more satisfying and make it a top Quest game.

old review after 2 days of play

I use Quest exclusively for cardio and some fun along the way, playing games like Pistol Whip, Beat Saber, and Fitxr over anything else. I've added Boombox to that list of fun games that burn a good amount of calories.

gameplay

As soon as I started a level I can feel how addictive it is to hit the drums to the beat, an hour flew by before I knew it. It is a different feeling than Beat Saber which has a slashing motion, compared to this game with a smashing motion, both satisfying in different ways. Every time you hit a drum, you get a sound effect, which you can change in settings, this adds to the beat of the song in a satisfying way. There are a good number of songs of various difficulties and much more made by the community, I would say the most difficult levels are not as hard as Beat Saber expert plus, and the easy levels might not be a challenge even for beginners who has played other games like this. Starting on normal or hard is a good idea for more fun. The only issue I have is the sound of the beat feels just a bit off, like a few milliseconds off of hitting the drum, but can't be sure if that's just me.

user interface

What stands out about this game is the cute user interface. It's very easy to navigate and pleasant to look at. There are many cartoony levels that are colourful and bright along with darker ones. You get a personal pet cat that runs ahead in game and stays in your room on the main menu, a very cute feature. You can also switch drum sticks, decorate your cat with hats and different skins, and earn achievements along with leaderboards for every level. I would like to see more leaderboard options outside of just the one times speed, like modifiers that increase scoring or speed that increases scoring for more competition. If this is implemented, please do it like Pistol Whip where every modifier combination as their own leaderboards, that will be endless playability to the game.

cardio

From testing for a few hours, I think this burns about the same as Beat Saber and a bit under Pistol Whip and Fitxr. For under 160lb, about 300 calories an hour can be burned, around 400 if you weigh over 160lb. It's a game that's so fun you won't think about the exercise you're doing. It requires mostly arm movements along with a bit of leg motion, like Beat Saber but a little less leg movement. There are obstacles to dodge so sometimes you will squat but it's not very often from what I've seen so far. This is a great main game to add to your exercise routine.

replay value

With leaderboards for all levels and every difficulty having a different leaderboard, achievements, decorations to unlock, there is a huge amount of replay value. What stands out is the community made maps. There are copyright free songs that users can use to make their own maps, this means we can all play hundreds of extra levels made by different people, giving us infinite new levels. I've seen some really difficult ones made already and they will be fun to beat when I get to that skill level. Every community map has its own leaderboard which is the most amazing feature. This is something no other game I've seen have on Quest so I thank the developers for this. Another bonus, since this is a new game, it's much easier to get top ten scores on the leaderboards, which is more fun for us score chasers compared to older games.

scoring

The scoring system in this game is very simple. Hit the note at the right time gives you full score, about when the note goes right below you. This means you don't have to aim for the center or slam down really far, because both those things won't affect the score. This makes this simpler to play than Beat Saber but it's also fun in that way because not having to worry about perfect scores. If you want a perfect high score, just keep a very consistent hit pattern when the note is close to you. I would like some new scoring systems because this will mean perfect scores on the leaderboards will be common. Maybe a system that rewards harder swings would increase the workout as well as scoring.

multiplayer

I haven't had a chance to try this because there aren't users in multiplayer so hopefully more players come in soon and spice it up. It looks like it might be a scoreboard only system where you see a board showing who's winning. A change to this would be good if they add other players on your map.

developer_response.body: Hi Ante_, thanks for your review, we are excited that you are enjoying our game!

reviews.review_description: Boombox is a very fun game and lately I've actually been enjoying it more than Beat Saber. The Quest port is top notch as well and doesn't feel like it needs any mods!

developer_response.body: Hi MindVR_, thanks for your review, we are excited that you are enjoying our game!

reviews.review_description: I first saw this game on LSToasts YouTube channel. I thought it was just another lousy Beat Saber rip-off, but I was wrong. When I first looked into the game I saw that it had in-game custom songs. Beat Saber doesn’t have built-in custom songs, so you get bored with it once you’ve beaten everything. However, BoomBox currently has 350+ custom songs for the community to map. This is the feature that makes this game a contender to Beat Saber. BoomBox does feel worse than Beat Saber in terms of satisfaction of hitting the notes, but that doesn’t necessarily mean it’s a bad game. Overall, BoomBox is one of my favorite games currently and has an awesome, expanding community. Now do yourself a favor and join the BoomBox community.

developer_response.body: Hi FlameWarrior, thanks for your review, we are excited that you are enjoying our game!
